study: Sangram SG-SE-004 (Аблатив и генитив) — cases + the genitive absolute vs the locative absolute
kind: beyond-quota core ① (opening set already 19/19); native positive; workflow probe+verify

snapshot.provenance_note: pin 04e0778 orphaned; binding = provenance table + SHA-256 + tag c3-pin-04e0778-content

ablative.note: source/comparison/cause; 92% singular. NB the top 'lemma' tva is the segmentation-layer -tva suffix token (H1178), not a lexical ablative; true ablative nouns include bhaya 'fear', hetu 'cause', kāla

genitive.pronoun_share_note: ~33% of genitives are pronouns (tad/mad/tvad 'of him/me/you') — the possessive genitive dominates
genitive.note: possession (adnominal) is the core; the genitive also took over the dative's recipient role (SG-SE-003 flip) and supplies the partitive/argument object (SG-SE-002 flip)

genitive_absolute_vs_locative.mirror_image_native_proof: on the deprel-parsed subsets the two cases are MIRROR images: Gen+Part is 7.6% adverbial(advcl*) / 63.4% attributive+adnominal(acl+nmod); Loc+Part is 64.7% adverbial / 7.3% attributive+adnominal — so the candidate near-parity is a provable artefact, not an assertion
genitive_absolute_vs_locative.finding: candidate near-parity (Gen+Part 16493 vs Loc+Part 20973) is an ARTEFACT — most Gen participles are attributive(acl 171)+adnominal(nmod 163), not absolute; adjudicated FLOOR (advcl* + subject-participle pairs, dedup overlap, computed identically) is 41 for the genitive absolute vs 405 for the locative — the genitive absolute is ~10x rarer. BUT the floor UNDERSTATES: the paśyat- 'disregard' idiom (dṛś, 373 Gen+Part) is the genitive absolute's semantic signature yet only 1 are deprel-parsed, so the true count is in the hundreds, not 41.

traditional_layer.witness: Whitney 1889 §§289-302 (ablative, genitive uses; ablative of comparison; genitive absolute); Pāṇini 2.3.28 (apādāna/abl), 2.3.50 (śeṣa/gen), 2.3.38 (ṣaṣṭhī sati-like genitive absolute)

limits.candidate_not_construction: Gen+Part (16493) is an upper bound for the genitive absolute; the adjudicated construction is ~41 (advcl* + pairs). Most Gen participles are attributive/adnominal.
limits.deprel_partial: the function split + absolute adjudication ride on the ~3-4%% dependency-parsed subset only
limits.comparison_not_native: ablative of comparison ('than X') is not a distinct native tag; obl:grad/context is a weak proxy — treated as traditional, not measured
limits.seg_artifact: the ablative -tva token (segmentation layer, H1178) inflates the raw lemma ranking; excluded from the 'true ablative noun' reading
limits.pin: orphaned 04e0778, bound by provenance table + SHA-256 + tag
